For example: 1.2 1.6||6.7 would display questions 1.2 and 1.6 if the first option is selected, none if the second is selected and 6.7 if the third option is selected)

column3: - Management: Ensures supplier relationships are approved, resourced, governed, and that risk acceptance and enforcement actions are authorized where required. - The Information Security function: Defines supplier security requirements, reviews supplier risk and assurance evidence, monitors compliance, and manages supplier-related security incidents, with the support of other department managers. - Business / Contract Owners: Own supplier relationships, ensure contracts include required information security clauses, and monitor supplier performance and compliance. - Procurement / Legal (where applicable): Ensures supplier agreements reflect legal, regulatory, contractual, and information security requirements. - Suppliers and Third Parties: Comply with all contractual and information security requirements and implement security controls within their scope of service. ---

column3: - Supplier relationships must be formally approved and risk assessed before engagement. - Information security requirements must be contractually defined and documented. - Suppliers must protect organizational information according to its classification. - Supplier compliance must be monitored throughout the relationship. - Information transfers to and from suppliers must be secured. - Changes to supplier services must not introduce unmanaged security risk. - Supplier access must be removed promptly upon termination or no longer being required. ---

column2: Standard - Supplier Agreements and Security Clauses
column3: Supplier agreements must include, where applicable: - Description of information accessed or provided and access methods. - Information classification and handling requirements. - Legal, regulatory, and contractual compliance obligations. - Defined security controls, including access control, monitoring, and logging. - Acceptable and unacceptable use of information and assets. - Authorization and removal of supplier personnel access. - Incident notification and collaboration requirements. - Sub-contracting restrictions and approval requirements. - Right to audit and assurance requirements. - Reporting obligations and remediation timelines. - Business continuity, backup, and disaster recovery requirements. - Change management requirements, including advance notification. - Termination, exit, and secure disposal obligations.

column3: - Information transfers must reflect the classification of the information. - Transfer methods must be approved and documented. - Controls must protect against interception, unauthorized access, loss, or alteration. - Responsibilities and liabilities for transfer-related incidents must be defined. #### Electronic Transfer - Sensitive information must be protected (e.g. encryption). - Malware protection must be implemented. - Strong authentication must be used over public networks. - Use of external public services (e.g. file sharing, messaging) requires approval. - Automatic forwarding to external addresses must be restricted. - SMS, instant messaging, and fax must not be used for sensitive information unless approved. #### Physical Media Transfer - Only approved couriers may be used. - Packaging must protect against damage and unauthorized access. - Tamper-evident controls must be used where required. - Transfers must be logged, including sender, recipient, and receipt confirmation. #### Verbal Information Transfer - Confidential discussions must not occur in public or insecure environments. - Participants must be authorized. - Appropriate room and environmental controls must be used. - Sensitive conversations must start with a classification disclaimer.
column5: Does this requirement reflect your company current practice: Information transfers must reflect the classification of the information.

column8: Policy document|Procedure document|Assigned owner or role record|IAM export|Access review records|User account listing|Approval tickets|Authentication logs|MFA configuration screenshots|Password manager records|Network diagrams|Firewall rule export|VPN configuration|Other

column3: - Information transfers must reflect the classification of the information. - Transfer methods must be approved and documented. - Controls must protect against interception, unauthorized access, loss, or alteration. - Responsibilities and liabilities for transfer-related incidents must be defined. #### Electronic Transfer - Sensitive information must be protected (e.g. encryption). - Malware protection must be implemented. - Strong authentication must be used over public networks. - Use of external public services (e.g. file sharing, messaging) requires approval. - Automatic forwarding to external addresses must be restricted. - SMS, instant messaging, and fax must not be used for sensitive information unless approved. #### Physical Media Transfer - Only approved couriers may be used. - Packaging must protect against damage and unauthorized access. - Tamper-evident controls must be used where required. - Transfers must be logged, including sender, recipient, and receipt confirmation. #### Verbal Information Transfer - Confidential discussions must not occur in public or insecure environments. - Participants must be authorized. - Appropriate room and environmental controls must be used. - Sensitive conversations must start with a classification disclaimer.

column3: - Security requirements must be defined before acquiring ICT products or services. - Suppliers must propagate security requirements to sub-suppliers. - Suppliers must provide: - Information on software and hardware components - Security functions and secure configuration guidance - Delivered products and services must be validated for compliance. - Critical components must be identifiable, traceable, and assured. - Measures must ensure components are genuine and untampered. - ICT lifecycle and availability risks must be actively managed.
